name: One-Arm Dumbbell Row
instructions: Place one knee and the same-side hand on a flat bench, keeping your back parallel to the floor. Hold a dumbbell in the opposite hand, arm extended straight down. Pull the dumbbell up to your hip, driving your elbow behind you. Squeeze your back muscle at the top. Lower the weight under control until your arm is fully extended.
form_cues: Keep your shoulders square to the bench
Don't twist your torso; isolate the back
Pull the dumbbell toward your hip, not your shoulder
safety_notes: Start with a light weight to master the form. Do not rotate your spine during the pull. If you have low-back issues, use an incline bench set to 45° for chest support.
common_mistakes: Twisting the torso to lift heavier weight
Pulling the dumbbell too high (shoulder instead of hip)
